# Kucoin.Net

> Strongly typed .NET client library for the Kucoin REST and WebSocket APIs. Supports Spot, Margin, High Frequency spot trading, Futures, Unified Account endpoints, and CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis integration.

Kucoin.Net provides typed REST and WebSocket clients, automatic request signing, Kucoin passphrase credentials, rate limiting, WebSocket reconnection, local order book support, native AOT support, and `WebCallResult<T>` / `CallResult<T>` error handling. Current version in this repository: 8.12.0. Targets netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, net10.0.

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/JKorf/Kucoin.Net/blob/master/README.md): Overview, installation, supported features, basic usage examples, release notes
- [Documentation Site](https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/Kucoin.Net/): Documentation hub for clients, WebSocket subscriptions, dependency injection, and shared interfaces
- [AI API Quick Map](https://github.com/JKorf/Kucoin.Net/blob/master/docs/ai-api-map.md): Table-style intent-to-method map for Spot, Futures, Unified, WebSocket, and SharedApis
- [Full LLM Context](https://github.com/JKorf/Kucoin.Net/blob/master/llms-full.txt): Detailed AI/LLM guide with endpoint routing, code patterns, and anti-hallucination rules

## Examples

- [AI-friendly examples directory](https://github.com/JKorf/Kucoin.Net/tree/master/Examples/ai-friendly): Compact, compilable examples optimized for AI assistants and quick onboarding
- [Spot Quickstart](https://github.com/JKorf/Kucoin.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/01-spot-quickstart.cs): Client setup, ticker, account balances, limit order, order status
- [Futures Trading](https://github.com/JKorf/Kucoin.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/02-futures.cs): Futures contract ticker, leverage parameter, order placement, positions, reduce-only close
- [WebSocket Streams](https://github.com/JKorf/Kucoin.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs): Spot ticker, klines, authenticated order and balance streams, teardown
- [Multi-Exchange Pattern](https://github.com/JKorf/Kucoin.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs): CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis for exchange-agnostic code
- [Error Handling](https://github.com/JKorf/Kucoin.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs): `WebCallResult` checks, retry logic, Kucoin-specific symbol and credentials pitfalls

## Critical Rules

- Use `KucoinRestClient` and `KucoinSocketClient`; do not generate raw HTTP calls.
- Use `KucoinCredentials("key", "secret", "passphrase")`; Kucoin needs the passphrase.
- Spot symbols are dash-separated, for example `BTC-USDT`.
- Futures symbols are contract names, for example `XBTUSDTM` or `ETHUSDTM`.
- Check `.Success` before `.Data` for every REST and socket result.
- Store `UpdateSubscription` values and unsubscribe with the concrete socket client.
- Inspect `Kucoin.Net/Interfaces/Clients/**` before generating endpoint names.

## Reference

- [CryptoExchange.Net base library](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoExchange.Net)
- [NuGet Package](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Kucoin.Net)
- [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/JKorf/Kucoin.Net/issues)
